title: C# Ternary Operator
content:
### Ternary Operator in C#

The ternary operator `?:` is a shorthand for simple if-else statements. It evaluates a condition and returns one of two expressions.

### Syntax

```csharp
condition ? expressionTrue : expressionFalse;
```

### Basic Example

```csharp
int age = 20;
string status = (age >= 18) ? "Adult" : "Minor";
Console.WriteLine(status);  // Adult
```

### Nested Ternary (Use with Caution)

```csharp
string grade = score >= 90 ? "A" :
               score >= 80 ? "B" :
               score >= 70 ? "C" :
               score >= 60 ? "D" : "F";
```

### Ternary vs if-else

| Aspect | Ternary | if-else |
|--------|---------|---------|
| Syntax | Single line | Multiple lines |
| Readability | Simple only | Complex logic |
| Return value | Returns value | Statement |
| Best for | Assignments | Multiple actions |

### Practical Examples

```csharp
// Minimum of two numbers
int min = (a < b) ? a : b;

// Conditional formatting
Console.WriteLine($"Status: {(isActive ? "Active" : "Inactive")}");

// Default value
string name = input ?? "default";  // Null coalescing (similar)

// Inline in expressions
int absolute = (x < 0) ? -x : x;
```

### Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Wrong | Correct |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Wrong type | `int x = (true) ? "hello" : 5;` | Both types must match |
| Side effects | `(true) ? a++ : b++;` | Prefer if-else for side effects |
| Over-nesting | Multi-level nesting | Use if-else or switch |

### Null Coalescing Operator (??)

Related operator for null checks:

```csharp
string? name = null;
string display = name ?? "Guest";  // "Guest"
```

> **Key Point:** The ternary operator is best for simple conditional assignments. Both expressions must have the same type (or be implicitly convertible). Avoid nesting ternaries for complex logic - use if-else or switch instead.

### Conditional Ref Operator (C# 7.2+)

```csharp
ref var r = ref (condition ? ref x : ref y);
r = 100;  // Assigns to x or y based on condition
```

quiz.question: What is the syntax of the ternary operator in C#?
What is a common limitation of the ternary operator?
options: Both expressions must have the same type
It cannot be used with numbers
